item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm only left with a single question:

Did the good boi get a new arm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a solid Series III vibe.

item.title: Amazed by this skip
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to be honest, I felt predisposed to a down vote for some strange reason. But the homages this article pays to the SCP's of old reignites the fire that blazed so passionately in my 13 year old self as I ran down the corridors of the first series.

I will not claim that this skip is anything near unique, by all means, the nitch of the anomaly-tinkerer has been filled beyond capacity. But the tone this article sets lets me look past the lack of originality. I approve of this skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lots of people were complaining about that, so I changed it. The reason I did it at first was because I wanted some objects to be in a place besides Dr. Sherry's possession.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP. It's interesting and it looks like you've put alot of thought into it. Though I would like to ask why they would give magnetic coins to the United States Department of Treasury.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find this incredibly flat and uninteresting, especially considering the decent day-one rating. I also question giving magnetic coins to the Treasury, not to mention "Dr. Toboggan" is one of those names that's really hard to take seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple and nice. Gonna be honest, and I hate to think that the pretty great phrase "The Joy of Creation" has been soiled by the game series but, when I saw the title on the mainlist the first thing I thought was Five Nights at Freddy's. Thankfully this wasn't a Five Nights at Freddy's article, and instead a metal man that likes metal and makes metal things.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup. I'm suprised nobody else caught that when I was going through drafts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Making the reader sympathize with 3425 was my main goal in writing this. The idea of a thing that makes other objects can be a bit overdone, so I decided to give mine a personality and a passion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did the Foundation give magnetic coins to the US Treasury?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice

Only small problem I have with this is the object class. Personally I think it should be Euclid because it's a humanoid

Otherwise, this is very good for a first time SCP

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP. It's cute, and original. made me feel bad for the thing when the arm came off. I enjoy it's creativity (scp itself, not towards the scp article).
+1 from me
